summary: 9 items surfaced from the day’s qualifying HN stories; strongest signals came from local AI tooling, remote KVM, and frontend frameworks.

items.name: oMLX
items.source_story_title: How to setup a local coding agent on macOS
items.summary: oMLX — macOS-native MLX inference server with SSD caching for local coding agents.
items.why_included: HN commenters treated it as the practical Mac setup for local agents.
items.evidence: The thread praised its model selection, fast downloads, and launching Claude Code and Codex on Apple Silicon.

items.name: Kimi K2.7-Code
items.source_story_title: Kimi K2.7-Code: open-source coding model with better token efficiency
items.summary: Kimi K2.7-Code — open-weight coding model tuned for long-horizon agentic work.
items.why_included: A commenter reported a real patch-rebase success with minimal prompting.
items.evidence: One commenter said it rebased a 177KB Fil-C OpenSSL patch from 3.3.1 to 3.5.7 with bare-bones instructions.

items.name: StackScope
items.source_story_title: Show HN: StackScope – I crawled over 40k indie launches to see what they ship
items.summary: StackScope — launch-scene stack intelligence for new product releases.
items.why_included: Commenters liked the product-analysis angle and wanted more launch visibility.
items.evidence: HN called the crawl of 40k indie launches interesting and discussed seeing their own launch report.

items.name: iPXE
items.source_story_title: Introduction to UEFI HTTP(s) Boot with QEMU/OVMF
items.summary: iPXE — open-source network boot firmware for HTTP/HTTPS boot and chainloading.
items.why_included: The UEFI thread treated it as the cleanest boot-path upgrade.
items.evidence: Commenters said to use iPXE, highlighted its HTTP/HTTPS boot features, and noted it can replace PXE ROMs.

items.name: JetKVM
items.source_story_title: Introduction to UEFI HTTP(s) Boot with QEMU/OVMF
items.summary: JetKVM — open-source KVM-over-IP box for remote machine control.
items.why_included: It was recommended as a concrete out-of-band control option.
items.evidence: The UEFI boot discussion suggested JetKVM alongside BMCs and iPXE for bypassing PXE/UEFI limits.

items.name: Comet (GL-RM1)
items.source_story_title: Introduction to UEFI HTTP(s) Boot with QEMU/OVMF
items.summary: Comet (GL-RM1) — GL.iNet's remote KVM for browser-based out-of-band access.
items.why_included: Commenters named it as another practical remote-control device.
items.evidence: The same UEFI thread listed GL.iNet Comet next to JetKVM as a way to control a machine remotely.

items.name: Tauri
items.source_story_title: Slightly reducing the sloppiness of AI generated front end
items.summary: Tauri — lightweight desktop app framework that keeps a web frontend small.
items.why_included: The front-end thread suggested it as the wrapper around AI-generated UI work.
items.evidence: One comment said to use Svelte and then wrap it up using Tauri.

items.name: Svelte
items.source_story_title: Slightly reducing the sloppiness of AI generated front end
items.summary: Svelte — concise frontend framework suggested for tighter AI-generated UI stacks.
items.why_included: Commenters used it as a concrete frontend stack instead of open-ended HTML generation.
items.evidence: The thread recommended Svelte plus Tauri as a more structured path for AI-made front ends.

items.name: GTK
items.source_story_title: Slightly reducing the sloppiness of AI generated front end
items.summary: GTK — cross-platform widget toolkit called out as a coherent UI style target.
items.why_included: It was explicitly mentioned as a style grammar that produces less generic output.
items.evidence: A commenter said they liked GTK, and the discussion favored specific design systems over vague “make it good” prompts.
